_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: User focus involves understanding the user needs to develop a detailed understanding of the problems that need to be solved.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in producing analysis documentation that is reproducible, clearly reasoned, and accessible to a non-technical business audience.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-S-010 (OP, cosine: 0.396), LS-S-005 (LS, cosine: 0.396).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.2/10 (raw 0.225). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DDaT-only terms [detailed, develop, focus, involves, needs, problems] vs WIDAI-only terms [accessible, analysis, audience, business, clearly, documentation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.861 (P=0.867, R=0.855). Balanced P/R — the 20-word DDaT element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "User focus involves understanding the user needs to develop a detailed understan" and "Skill in producing analysis documentation that is reproducible, clearly reasoned" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.357 > STS 0.225 (gap +0.132). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.7%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DDaT terms: [detailed, develop, focus, involves, needs, problems]. WIDAI terms: [accessible, analysis, audience, business, clearly, documentation].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.132 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
